tldr: Checkmarx is an enterprise SAST/SCA/IaC platform, while Jit is a developer-security orchestration layer that wires up open-source scanners (SAST, SCA, secrets, IaC) into a product-security plan. Migrating means trading a heavyweight, integrated suite for an opinionated orchestrator over OSS engines.
why_switch: Shift from enterprise SAST to a lightweight orchestrator for OSS scanners
Improve developer ergonomics in GitHub workflows
Get SCA, secrets, and IaC coverage in one product-security plan
what_to_export: List of Checkmarx projects and underlying repositories
Open and historical findings, ideally as SARIF
export_method: Export findings via Checkmarx's SARIF export where available, and archive scan reports for audit retention. Document custom queries separately — they are unlikely to translate to Jit's orchestrated OSS engines, so they must be reimplemented as engine-specific rules or accepted as a gap. Capture risk-acceptance decisions in a structured list for re-application. Treat user/role and CI integration data as documentation; recreate them natively in Jit. Do not move credentials directly.
import_method: Connect Jit to your source-control organisation (typically GitHub) and let it discover repositories. Jit composes a security plan from OSS engines (e.g. Semgrep, Gitleaks, KICS, Trivy) per repository; turn on the controls that map to the Checkmarx scan types you used (SAST, SCA, secrets, IaC). Re-apply suppressions per engine, since Jit defers to the underlying scanners' ignore mechanisms. Tune PR-blocking thresholds conservatively for the first sprint to avoid build failures, then ratchet them up as developers triage backlog. Keep Checkmarx running in parallel until coverage parity is verified per repository.

concept_mapping.target: OSS SAST engine (e.g. Semgrep) orchestrated by Jit
OSS SCA engine orchestrated by Jit

gotchas: Checkmarx custom queries do not transfer; reimplement against the underlying OSS engine or drop them
Engine-level coverage may differ from Checkmarx's proprietary analyzers — validate per language
Historical scan data stays in Checkmarx; archive reports before cancellation
Enterprise contracts may have notice periods — confirm before scheduling a cutover
PR-blocking on day one will likely fail builds; phase in gates
Suppressions are per-engine, not centralised, so audit them carefully

email.subject: Moving application security from Checkmarx to Jit
email.body:
Hi team,

Over the next 14 days we are migrating our application security tooling from Checkmarx to Jit, which orchestrates OSS scanners on each repository. Both tools will run in parallel until we have validated coverage and tuned PR gates per repo. Expect new check names in your pull requests during this period. Please reply if you depend on a specific Checkmarx report so we can plan its replacement.

Thanks for bearing with us.

Your customer
